records.ety: [F. effacer; pref. es- (L. ex) + face face; prop., to destroy the face or form. See Face, and cf. Deface.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Effaced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Effacing (?).]

defs: To cause to disappear (as anything impresses or inscribed upon a surface) by rubbing out, striking out, etc.; to erase; to render illegible or indiscernible; as, to efface the letters on a monument, or the inscription on a coin.
To destroy, as a mental impression; to wear away.
quotes: Efface from his mind the theories and notions vulgarly received.
synonyms: Deface. To deface is to injure or impair a figure
to efface is to rub out or destroy
